title: FWM Windows Git, S3, IAM, and Transcript Update - 2026-04-28

messages.text: Asked Codex to use Git and S3 to make sure FWM_Repo and FWM_Data were up to date, and to check the transcripts table for recent Codex conversations from another laptop.
Located FWM_Repo and FWM_Data under C:\Users\bsing\OneDrive\Documents\Projects. Confirmed FWM_Repo is a Git repo and FWM_Data is a sibling data directory. Found AWS CLI and Git installed.

Fetched origin/main after elevated Git access was needed for .git/FETCH_HEAD. Fast-forwarded FWM_Repo to origin/main commit 5cf2861749c2af13eb0789f231180de0f2355a8e while preserving existing local uncommitted edits through a stash/pop workflow.

Read Supabase codex_chat_transcripts metadata through the project REST API. Recent rows included Non-Amazon scraping/S3 handoff, Sovrn Commerce CTR tracking, and S3 backup/AWS credential rotation conversations from April 27-28, 2026.
AWS profile fwm initially had an expired root login session and default credentials were invalid. Opened a normal PowerShell AWS login flow so the user could complete browser authentication.
Asked what to do when PowerShell printed an AWS sign-in URL.
Instructed the user to copy the AWS sign-in URL into a normal browser, complete login, then return to PowerShell so aws sts get-caller-identity could verify the session.
Reported that the AWS login was done.

Verified AWS profile fwm as arn:aws:iam::326804802943:root, then restored FWM_Data from s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger using a non-destructive aws s3 sync. Downloaded about 447 MiB of newer/missing data. Verified no remaining download changes with a dry run.
Reported FWM_Repo was at latest origin/main and FWM_Data had been restored. Noted that AWS was still using root and recommended switching fwm to a safer IAM user.
Asked to deal with the IAM thing and what the user had to do.
Inspected IAM with the root-authenticated fwm profile. Found existing IAM user codex-sync. Found it had broad AmazonS3FullAccess and one active access key from 2026-04-27.

Replaced codex-sync broad AmazonS3FullAccess with bucket-scoped inline policy FWMDataBucketSyncPolicy for s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger. Created a fresh local access key for Windows and configured AWS profile fwm to use codex-sync access keys instead of root login_session.

Verified aws --profile fwm sts get-caller-identity now returns arn:aws:iam::326804802943:user/codex-sync. Verified bucket listing and a dry-run backup with the IAM user. Uploaded seven pending local FWM_Data files to S3. Left the older active codex-sync key alone because it may still be used by the other laptop.
Updated AWS_BACKUP_SETUP.md and S3_RESTORE_HANDOFF.md to document that fwm now uses IAM user codex-sync with bucket-scoped permissions, not root.
Asked to update the transcripts table and any relevant documentation files in the repo.

Updated DATA.md to remove stale root login guidance and document expected codex-sync identity. Updated CODEX_CHAT_TRANSCRIPTS.md to explain focused handoff transcript JSONs when a full Codex desktop export is not available. Created this session transcript JSON and prepared to upload it to Supabase codex_chat_transcripts.
